In Time (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $37,553,932Details
International Box Office $127,550,020
Worldwide Box Office $165,103,952
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $9,970,355
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $7,539,556
Total $17,509,911
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When Will Salas is falsely accused of murder, he must figure out a way to bring down a system where time is money - literally - enabling the wealthy to live forever while the poor, like Will, have to beg, borrow, and steal enough minutes to make it through another day.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,050,368 (32.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.12 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:22.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$35,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,122 opening theaters/3,127 max. theaters, 4.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $56,636,006

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 28th, 2011 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: November 3rd, 2011 (Wide) (Slovakia)
Video Release: January 31st, 2012 by Fox Home Entertainment
May 21st, 2012 by 20th Century Fox
MPA Rating: PG-13 for violence, some sexuality and partial nudity, and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2189, 9/14/2011)
Running Time: 109 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Digital Cinematography, Prologue, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Corrupt Cops, Fugitive / On the Run, Gangs, Suicide
Social setting: Dystopia
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Regency Enterprises, New Regency, Strike Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

When Will Salas is falsely accused of murder, he must figure out a way to bring down a system where time is money - literally - enabling the wealthy to live forever while the poor, like Will, have to beg, borrow, and steal enough minutes to make it through another day.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,050,368 (32.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.12 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:22.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$35,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,122 opening theaters/3,127 max. theaters, 4.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $56,636,006

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 28th, 2011 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: November 3rd, 2011 (Wide) (Slovakia)
Video Release: January 31st, 2012 by Fox Home Entertainment
May 21st, 2012 by 20th Century Fox
MPA Rating: PG-13 for violence, some sexuality and partial nudity, and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2189, 9/14/2011)
Running Time: 109 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Digital Cinematography, Prologue, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Corrupt Cops, Fugitive / On the Run, Gangs, Suicide
